message:
feat(core,planner,vdbe): thread QualifiedName-based TableStar through JOIN + planner + codegen + cursor registry

Follow-ups to the earlier AST/parser/core changes in this batch
that taught FrankenSQLite to parse and resolve
`schema.table.*` result columns.  The initial commits handled
the AST shape (ResultColumn::TableStar(QualifiedName)), the
parser lookahead, and Connection::select_result_column_names.
This commit closes out the remaining call sites that still
assumed bare-string TableStar and fixes one VDBE engine
bug that prevented cursor reuse from picking up the collation
metadata the new unique-probe paths need.

crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs

- JOIN expansion path (~line 35316): the single-SELECT source
  walker used to pass `&[]` for visible_ctes to
  select_result_column_names, which meant a subquery source
  inside a JOIN couldn't resolve WITH-bound CTEs that shadowed
  an outer table name.  Now it computes
    let visible_ctes = select.with.as_ref().map_or_else(
        Vec::new,
        |with| with.ctes.clone(),
    );
  at the top and threads it through every call to
  select_result_column_names and col_map_for_source so the
  enclosing WITH clause propagates into the JOIN source
  resolution.

- The same change is applied to the two ~35363 call sites that
  expand a JOIN's right-hand Subquery source.

crates/fsqlite-planner/src/lib.rs

- Import QualifiedName from fsqlite_ast.
- resolve_single_table_result_columns_with_options() previously
  treated the ResultColumn::TableStar qualifier as an opaque
  &str and called qualifier_matches_table with a bare name.
  The qualifier is now a QualifiedName, so the function:
    - Checks qualifier.schema against the table's schema (when
      set) — a `main.users.*` TableStar against a `users` table
      in the `main` schema matches; against the `aux` schema
      it does not.
    - Falls back to the existing alias/name match when
      qualifier.schema is None.
- Error reporting: use qualifier.to_string() instead of a bare
  cloned String so the error message spells `schema.table.*`
  rather than just `schema.table`.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs

- resolve_join_output_count() (~line 6592): replace the old
  `alias.map_or_else(|| t.name.eq_ignore_ascii_case(name), |a|
  a.eq_ignore_ascii_case(name))` pattern with the QualifiedName-
  aware check:
    (name.schema.is_none()
        && alias.is_some_and(|a| a.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&name.name)))
        || t.name.eq_ignore_ascii_case(&name.name)
  so the join codegen correctly counts output columns for
  schema-qualified TableStars.

- emit_join_result_columns() (~line 6795): same treatment for
  the TableStar arm that actually emits the per-column reads.
  Drop the intermediate `name_lower` string because the match
  now compares via eq_ignore_ascii_case directly against
  &table_name.name.

- emit_column_reads() (~line 11948) and
  resolve_result_column_indices() (~line 13005): both were
  doing `&qualifier.clone()` or `qualifier.eq_ignore_ascii_case(
  &table.name)` against a String. Update to read
  `qualifier.name` (the bare table portion of the QualifiedName)
  and emit the full qualifier via qualifier.to_string() in the
  TableNotFound error variant.

- Codegen unit test fixture update: the one test that
  constructed a ResultColumn::TableStar("u".to_owned()) now
  uses ResultColumn::TableStar(QualifiedName::bare("u")).

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs

- Bug fix: cursor_root_pages was not updated when a cursor was
  reused after an OpenRead / OpenWrite against the same root.
  The earlier commits that look up collation metadata via
  `self.cursor_root_pages.get(&cursor_id).copied()` (both the
  NoConflict and the IdxInsert collation-aware paths) would
  miss that entry whenever the cursor had been reused rather
  than freshly opened, falling back to the bytewise fast path
  even for NOCASE/RTRIM indexes.
- Add `self.cursor_root_pages.insert(cursor_id, root_page);`
  on every path where the engine records or updates a cursor's
  root page: the reuse path for OpenRead (~line 12721), the
  fresh-open branches in OpenRead (~line 12870), OpenWrite
  (~line 12977), and the rebind branch (~line 13102).
- New unit test exercises a cursor reuse + NOCASE unique
  insert sequence to prove the collation registry is
  consulted after reuse.

No changes to public API; the changes are all in internal
helpers that the new schema-qualified TableStar shape needed
to understand.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
